new_classes.label_en: Wine with PGI in containers > 2 L with actual alcoholic strength ≤ 15% vol (CN 2204 29 80)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.g.A. in Behältern > 2 L mit einem tatsächlichen Alkoholgehalt von ≤ 15 % vol (KN 2204 29 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres and with an actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 80 are: (1) wine of fresh grapes, (2) bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) per Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, (3) in containers greater than 2 litres, and (4) actual alcoholic strength not exceeding 15% vol (the code is the complement of the > 15% vol subheading 2204 29 79 within PGI large-container wines). These are captured by the PGIOriginQuality restriction, the LargeContainer parthood restriction, the alcoholByVolumePercent maxInclusive 15.0 range restriction, and the complement of PDOOriginQuality to distinguish from PDO wines. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because the specific content of Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 (the legal definition of PGI wines) is not fully formalised within the ontology — it is referenced but not detailed — and because the lower bound of the alcohol range (distinguishing from semi-sparkling wines) is not explicitly modelled here.
